params.title: Schedule cron
params.description:
Cron expression consists of five subexpressions separated by a blank: minute, 
hour, day of month, month, day of week. Node allows you to run the scenario by cron expression in the selected time zone. 
Note: the "Run once" command will not run a scenario by schedule! To start the node, it must be deployed on prod 
environment and be \"Active\". To disable the node, deactivate the script or remove the node from the script and deploy 
the updated script to prod environment.

params.title: Response Time (ms)
params.description: Optional: Provide the response time from workflow logs or another node

params.title: To
params.description: Enter a single recipient's email or multiple emails as items in an array

params.title: Subject
params.description: Specify a subject for the email

params.title: Email Body
params.description: Include an email body as either plain text or HTML. If HTML, make sure to set the "Body Type" prop to html

params.title: Body Type
params.description: Choose to send as plain text or HTML

params.title: Cc
params.description: Enter a single recipient's email or multiple emails as items in an array

params.title: Bcc
params.description: Enter a single recipient's email or multiple emails as items in an array

params.title: From Name
params.description: Specify the name that will be displayed in the "From" section of the email

params.title: Message ID To Reply
params.description: Specify the `message ID` this email is replying to

params.title: Attachments
params.description: Add any attachments you'd like to include as objects. The `key` should be the **filename** and the value should be the **url** for the attachment. The **filename** must contain the file extension (i.e. `.jpeg`, `.txt`) and the **url** is the download link for the file.

params.title: Quick preview schema
params.description:
This field lets you format response data for easy reading. Define key-path pairs in JSON, where the key is the header and the value is the data path.

Example: `{ "Name": "result.user.name", "Email": "result.contact.email" }`
